subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

formulas.name: Atomic Number
formulas.notes: The atomic number is a unique identifier for each element and determines its position in the periodic table
formulas.variables: Z is the atomic number, which is the number of protons in an atom's nucleus

formulas.name: Mass Number
formulas.notes: The mass number is the sum of protons and neutrons in an atom
formulas.variables: A is the mass number, Z is the atomic number, and N is the number of neutrons in an atom

formulas.name: Electron Configuration
formulas.notes: Electron configuration is a way to describe the arrangement of electrons in an atom
formulas.variables: The superscript number indicates the number of electrons in the corresponding subshell

formulas.name: Periodic Table Trends
formulas.notes: The periodic table shows trends in atomic radius, electronegativity, and other properties as you move horizontally or vertically
formulas.formula: As you move down a group, the atomic radius increases, and electronegativity decreases

formulas.name: Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR) Theory
formulas.notes: VSEPR theory explains the shape of molecules based on the repulsion between electron pairs
formulas.formula: The shape of a molecule is determined by the arrangement of electron pairs around the central atom

formulas.name: Electronegativity
formulas.notes: Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract electrons in a covalent bond
formulas.variables: X_A is the electronegativity of element A, E_A^+ is the ionization energy of element A, E_e^- is the electron affinity of element A, and E_e is the electron affinity of the electron

formulas.name: Ionization Energy
formulas.notes: Ionization energy is the energy required to remove an electron from an atom
formulas.variables: IE is the ionization energy, e is the charge of the electron, and V is the potential difference

formulas.name: Electron Affinity
formulas.notes: Electron affinity is the energy released when an electron is added to an atom
formulas.variables: EA is the electron affinity, e is the charge of the electron, and V is the potential difference

formulas.name: Hofmann's Rule
formulas.notes: Hofmann's rule describes the trend of electronegativity across the periodic table
formulas.formula: Electronegativity increases across a period, and decreases down a group

formulas.name: Mendeleev's Periodic Law
formulas.notes: Mendeleev's periodic law describes the arrangement of elements in the periodic table
formulas.formula: Elements are arranged in a periodic table based on their atomic weights and chemical properties

formulas.name: Modern Periodic Law
formulas.notes: The modern periodic law describes the arrangement of elements in the periodic table, taking into account atomic numbers and electron configurations
formulas.formula: Elements are arranged in a periodic table based on their atomic numbers and electron configurations
